What is the worst-case lookup time? What is the worst-case space usage?
A hash table is a data structure that uses a hash function to map keys to indices in an array. The hash function is used to convert the key into a numerical value that can be used as an index into the array. The array is then used to store the values associated with the keys.
The average lookup time for a hash table is O(1) because the hash function is used to quickly map the key to an index in the array. This means that the lookup time is independent of the number of keys in the hash table.
The worst-case lookup time for a hash table is O(n) because the hash function may map all keys to the same index in
